channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Mid-Career Professionals in Canada
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Aga Khan Foundation Canada (AKFC) Global Leadership Program 2026 is a transformative leadership development initiative designed to equip mid-career professionals in Canada with the knowledge, skills, and global perspective required to navigate today’s complex and interconnected world. As global challenges such as climate change, inequality, geopolitical instability, and social fragmentation continue to shape societies, […]

The post Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Mid-Career Professionals in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Aga Khan Foundation Canada (AKFC) Global Leadership Program 2026 is a transformative leadership development initiative designed to equip mid-career professionals in Canada with the knowledge, skills, and global perspective required to navigate today’s complex and interconnected world.

As global challenges such as climate change, inequality, geopolitical instability, and social fragmentation continue to shape societies, there is a growing need for leaders who can think beyond borders and collaborate across sectors. The AKFC Global Leadership Program responds to this need by creating a platform where professionals can deepen their understanding of global issues, enhance their leadership capacity, and contribute meaningfully to building a more inclusive, peaceful, and sustainable future.

Applications are now open for the 2026 cohort, offering a unique opportunity for Canadian professionals to engage in high-level discussions, experiential learning, and global networking.

About the Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program

The Global Leadership Program is a flagship initiative of the Aga Khan Foundation Canada aimed at:

  • Developing globally minded leaders
  • Strengthening leadership capacity across sectors
  • Promoting pluralism, collaboration, and sustainable development
  • Encouraging participants to explore Canada’s role in addressing global challenges

The program brings together a diverse cohort of professionals from different industries and backgrounds, creating a dynamic learning environment where participants exchange ideas, share experiences, and co-create solutions to pressing global issues.

Program Objectives

The AKFC Global Leadership Program is designed to:

  1. Enhance Global Awareness
    • Provide insights into global systems, trends, and challenges
    • Explore the interconnected nature of modern societies
  2. Strengthen Leadership Skills
    • Develop strategic thinking and decision-making abilities
    • Build resilience and adaptability in complex environments
  3. Promote Collaborative Problem-Solving
    • Encourage cross-sectoral dialogue and partnerships
    • Foster collective approaches to global challenges
  4. Empower Purpose-Driven Leadership
    • Help participants align personal values with global impact
    • Inspire action toward building inclusive and sustainable communities

Program Structure and Format

The Global Leadership Program combines virtual learning with an immersive in-person experience, ensuring both flexibility and deep engagement.

1. Virtual Learning Phase (September – October 2026)

Participants will engage in:

  • Peer learning group coaching
  • Interactive virtual sessions
  • Discussions with program alumni
  • Engagement with global thought leaders

This phase allows participants to build foundational knowledge, connect with peers, and prepare for the in-person residency.

2. In-Person Residency in Ottawa (November 1–6, 2026)

A key highlight of the program is the fully funded in-person residency in Ottawa, Canada.

During this residency, participants will:

  • Engage in intensive leadership workshops
  • Participate in simulations (e.g., global policy scenarios such as G20 simulations)
  • Interact with facilitators, guest speakers, and experts
  • Collaborate on real-world global challenges

All costs related to:

  • Travel within Canada
  • Accommodation
  • Meals

are fully covered by the program.

Key Program Themes

Participants will explore a wide range of global issues, including:

  • Climate action and environmental sustainability
  • Global politics and governance
  • Economic development and inequality
  • Social inclusion and pluralism
  • Canada’s role in global affairs

These themes are explored through a combination of lectures, discussions, simulations, and collaborative exercises.

Important Dates

Applicants should take note of the following timeline:

  • Application Opens: April 21, 2026
  • Application Deadline: May 19, 2026 (11:59 PM ET)
  • Shortlisting & Video Submission: Late June – Early July 2026
  • Final Selection Notification: By July 31, 2026
  • Virtual Program Start: September 16, 2026
  • Virtual Sessions: September – October 2026
  • In-Person Residency (Ottawa): November 1–6, 2026

Program Benefits

Participants selected for the AKFC Global Leadership Program will gain access to a wide range of benefits:

1. Fully Funded Experience

  • Travel within Canada covered
  • Accommodation provided
  • Meals included during residency

2. Leadership Development

  • Exposure to global leadership frameworks
  • Training in strategic thinking and problem-solving

3. Networking Opportunities

  • Access to a diverse cohort of professionals
  • Connections with alumni, experts, and global leaders

4. Experiential Learning

  • Participation in simulations and case studies
  • Hands-on engagement with global challenges

5. Long-Term Impact

  • Membership in a growing alumni network
  • Continued collaboration and learning opportunities

Eligibility Criteria

The program is designed for:

  • Mid-career Canadian professionals
  • Individuals from diverse sectors (public, private, non-profit, etc.)
  • Professionals with a demonstrated commitment to leadership and social impact

Applicants should have:

  • Strong interest in global issues
  • Commitment to collaboration and learning
  • Willingness to participate fully in both virtual and in-person components

Testimonials from Past Participants

Participants from previous cohorts have highlighted the program’s transformative impact:

  • It provides clarity of purpose and strengthens leadership direction
  • It offers exposure to diverse perspectives and ideas
  • It builds a strong community of changemakers
  • It inspires actionable insights for real-world challenges

Many alumni describe the program as a defining experience in their leadership journey.

How to Apply

Interested applicants must submit their applications within the official application window.

Application Process Overview:

  1. Submit an online application
  2. Await shortlisting notification
  3. Submit a short video interview response (if shortlisted)
  4. Receive final decision

APPLY HERE

For inquiries and support, applicants can contact:

  • Email: AKFC-GlobalLeadershipProgram@akdn.org

Conclusion

The Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026 offers a powerful opportunity for mid-career professionals to grow as leaders, expand their global perspective, and contribute meaningfully to solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

With a fully funded residency, world-class learning experiences, and access to a strong network of changemakers, this program stands out as one of the most impactful leadership opportunities available in Canada.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline and take a step toward shaping a more inclusive and sustainable future.

Click HERE to learn more about the Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026.

Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post Aga Khan Foundation Canada Global Leadership Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Mid-Career Professionals in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Open Global Power 2026: Fully-Funded Global Energy Leadership Programme in Italy for Emerging Professionals
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The global energy sector is undergoing a profound transformation, driven by the urgent need for sustainability, innovation, and inclusivity. In response to this shift, the Open Global Power (OGP) 2026 Programme, launched by the Enel Foundation, offers a unique and comprehensive opportunity for young professionals to become leaders in the energy transition. This prestigious initiative […]

The post Open Global Power 2026: Fully-Funded Global Energy Leadership Programme in Italy for Emerging Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global energy sector is undergoing a profound transformation, driven by the urgent need for sustainability, innovation, and inclusivity. In response to this shift, the Open Global Power (OGP) 2026 Programme, launched by the Enel Foundation, offers a unique and comprehensive opportunity for young professionals to become leaders in the energy transition.

This prestigious initiative builds on the success of previous regional programmes and expands into a global platform for advanced training, policy dialogue, and international collaboration. If you are passionate about shaping the future of energy systems, this programme could be a defining step in your career.

Programme Overview

The Open Global Power 2026 programme is designed to:

  • Equip participants with technical, regulatory, and business expertise in sustainable energy
  • Foster a global network of energy professionals and future leaders
  • Promote inclusive, innovation-driven energy transitions across regions
  • Bridge the gap between global best practices and local implementation

Participants will engage in a blended learning journey, combining online coursework with a potential in-person experience in Italy.

Programme Objectives

Open Global Power 2026 has been designed with the following core goals:

  1. Build a global community of current and future energy leaders by connecting professionals from across Africa, Latin America, and beyond
  2. Provide an integrated understanding of energy systems, covering regulation, economics, technology, and geopolitics
  3. Strengthen participants’ capacity to translate global best practices into solutions that are viable in their own local or regional contexts
  4. Foster cross-regional dialogue and peer learning among professionals with diverse professional, cultural, and geographic backgrounds
  5. Promote gender balance, geographic diversity, and merit-based selection throughout every stage of the programme

Programme Structure

The Open Global Power 2026 programme is built around four carefully designed and sequenced modules. Each module serves a distinct purpose in building participants’ knowledge, skills, and professional networks.

1. Foundational Module (Online)

  • Duration: 4 weeks (approx. 6 hours/week)
  • Focus Areas:
    • Energy systems and electricity markets
    • Energy economics and geopolitics
    • Power technologies and innovation
    • Digital tools for energy transition
  • Assessment:
    • Online multiple-choice test (First Assignment)

2. Global Policy and Regulation Module (Online)

  • Duration: ~4 months (June to mid-October)
  • Format: Self-paced + live sessions
  • Key Highlights:
    • Design of energy policies and regulatory frameworks
    • Understanding sustainable energy access strategies
    • Completion of a Country Project (individual or group)
  • Certification Levels:
    • Certificate of Attendance
    • Certificate of Completion
    • Certificate of Excellence

3. Territorial Modules (Online)

  • Duration: 4 weeks (4–8 hours/week)
  • Focus:
    • Regional energy challenges and solutions
    • Infrastructure and regulatory frameworks
    • Socio-economic and environmental considerations
    • Real-world case studies
  • Assessment:
    • Second online multiple-choice test (Second Assignment)

4. Final Residential Module in Italy (In-Person)

  • Duration: Approximately 1 week
  • Eligibility: Top 10% of participants
  • Activities Include:
    • Advanced lectures and workshops
    • Group policy discussions
    • Site visits
    • Networking with global experts

Give Back Opportunity

Graduates may be invited to contribute to future educational initiatives and events organized by the Enel Foundation.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Age: 24–35 years (as of April 13, 2026)
  • Citizenship: African country, Brazil, or Chile
  • Education:
    • Must hold a Master’s, MBA, or PhD
    • At least one degree from Africa, Brazil, or Chile
  • Professional Interest:
    • Demonstrated commitment to a career in energy or electricity sectors
  • Language Requirement:
    • Advanced English proficiency (CEFR C1 level)
  • Mobility:
    • Must be eligible to travel to Italy

Application Process

To apply, candidates must submit the following documents (in English):

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Academic certificates and transcripts
  • Passport copy
  • Motivation letter (max 700 words)
  • Two reference letters

Important Notes:

  • Applications must be submitted via the official BeeZ platform
  • Only one application per candidate is allowed
  • Incomplete applications will not be accepted
  • Once submitted, applications cannot be edited

Selection Process

The selection is highly competitive and merit-based, involving:

  • Academic and professional evaluation
  • Review of motivation and reference letters

Participant Allocation:

  • 100 participants from Africa
  • 60 participants from Brazil
  • 40 participants from Chile

Top-performing participants from the online phase will be selected for the final Italy residency based on:

  • Assignment performance
  • Engagement throughout the programme

Application Deadline

  • Opening Date: April 13, 2026
  • Closing Date: May 15, 2026 (23:59 CEST)

Late applications will not be considered.

Why You Should Apply

Joining Open Global Power 2026 offers:

  • Access to world-class training and academic partnerships
  • Exposure to global energy policies and innovations
  • Opportunity to network with international experts and peers
  • A pathway to becoming a leader in the global energy transition
  • Potential fully-funded experience in Italy

How to Apply

Applicants must apply using the application code:

  • Code: OAPF1397

Click here to learn more and apply

NB. Ensure all documents are prepared and submitted before the deadline to avoid disqualification.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities here

The post Open Global Power 2026: Fully-Funded Global Energy Leadership Programme in Italy for Emerging Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Innovations for Poverty Action Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Research for Sustainable Impact
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI), a collaborative effort by Innovations for Poverty Action (IPA) and the Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL), has officially launched its Fifth Call for Proposals (Round V). This funding opportunity is designed to support high-quality research and innovative solutions aimed at improving livelihoods for displaced populations and host communities […]

The post Innovations for Poverty Action Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Research for Sustainable Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI), a collaborative effort by Innovations for Poverty Action (IPA) and the Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL), has officially launched its Fifth Call for Proposals (Round V). This funding opportunity is designed to support high-quality research and innovative solutions aimed at improving livelihoods for displaced populations and host communities worldwide.

Backed by funding from the IKEA Foundation, the initiative seeks to generate actionable insights that influence policies, strengthen programs, and create sustainable economic opportunities for some of the world’s most vulnerable populations.

Overview of the Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI)

The DLI focuses on funding projects that provide rigorous, evidence-based insights into what works in improving livelihoods for displaced populations, including refugees and internally displaced persons (IDPs).

Types of Projects Supported

DLI supports a wide range of research and programmatic efforts, including:

  • Impact Evaluations
    Rigorous studies that measure the effectiveness of interventions.
  • Pilot Projects
    Early-stage initiatives testing innovative ideas before scaling.
  • Exploratory Studies
    Research that helps identify new pathways or hypotheses.
  • Infrastructure and Public Goods Projects
    Initiatives that build systems or tools benefiting broader communities.

New Additions in Round V

Round V introduces two new funding categories aimed at expanding participation and knowledge dissemination:

  1. Evidence Use Grants
    Designed to help policymakers and practitioners apply research findings effectively.
  2. Conference Travel Grants
    Supporting researchers—especially those with lived experience of displacement—to present findings and engage with global audiences.

Additionally, a portion of funding is specifically reserved for:

  • Researchers with lived experience of displacement
  • Projects partnering with refugee-led organizations

This ensures that research is not only inclusive but also grounded in real-world experiences.

Key Research Priority Areas

DLI encourages proposals that address critical issues affecting displaced populations and host communities. Priority themes include:

  • Resilience
    Strengthening the ability of individuals and communities to adapt and recover.
  • Entrepreneurship
    Supporting small businesses and self-employment opportunities.
  • Wage Employment
    Improving access to stable and fair employment.
  • Social Cohesion, Inclusion, and Norms
    Promoting harmonious coexistence between displaced and host communities.
  • Rights and Regulations
    Understanding and improving legal and policy frameworks affecting livelihoods.

Application Process Explained

Unlike previous rounds, submission of an Expression of Interest (EOI) is mandatory for all applicants.

Step-by-Step Application Flow

  1. Submit Expression of Interest (EOI)
    • Required to proceed to full proposal stage
    • Provides early feedback from DLI reviewers
  2. EOI Review and Feedback
    • Selected applicants receive detailed feedback
    • Only shortlisted teams are invited to submit full proposals
  3. Full Proposal Submission
    • Comprehensive project plan submitted via IPA
  4. Final Selection and Award Announcement

Important Dates (Round V)

  • Mid-April 2026: Call for Proposals Announced
  • May 15, 2026: Mandatory Expression of Interest Deadline
  • Week of June 8, 2026: EOI Decisions Released
  • July 24, 2026: Full Proposal Submission Deadline
  • September 2026: Award Winners Announced

Note: EOIs submitted after the deadline may still be accepted, but will not receive detailed feedback, reducing chances of success.

Why Apply for the DLI Grant?

Participating in the DLI Call for Proposals offers several benefits:

  • Access to Funding for impactful and scalable research
  • Global Recognition through collaboration with IPA and J-PAL
  • Policy Influence by contributing to evidence-based decision-making
  • Networking Opportunities with global researchers and practitioners
  • Capacity Building through exposure to rigorous research methodologiesAPPLY HERE

Additional Resources for Applicants

Applicants are encouraged to explore the following before applying:

  • Application Instructions
  • Eligibility Quiz
  • Proposal Tips
  • Round V Changes Overview
  • Previously Funded Projects

Information sessions (recorded) are also available for both researchers and policymakers to better understand the application process.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Innovations for Poverty Action Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Research for Sustainable Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Justice Conservation Fund: Empowering Communities to Protect Forests and Build Sustainable Futures
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Justice Conservation Fund (JCF) is a transformative initiative designed to protect forests while empowering under-resourced communities across the Southern Coastal Blackbelt. Built on a partnership between Dogwood Alliance and New Alpha Community Development Corporation, this fund is redefining conservation by placing ownership, decision-making, and economic opportunity directly in the hands of local communities. At […]

The post Justice Conservation Fund: Empowering Communities to Protect Forests and Build Sustainable Futures appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Conservation Fund (JCF) is a transformative initiative designed to protect forests while empowering under-resourced communities across the Southern Coastal Blackbelt. Built on a partnership between Dogwood Alliance and New Alpha Community Development Corporation, this fund is redefining conservation by placing ownership, decision-making, and economic opportunity directly in the hands of local communities.

At its core, the Justice Conservation Fund is not just about preserving land—it is about restoring justice, strengthening communities, and creating sustainable economic pathways rooted in environmental stewardship.

What is the Justice Conservation Fund?

The Justice Conservation Fund provides financial support to rural communities—particularly Black and Indigenous populations—to:

  • Purchase forest land
  • Retain ownership and control of natural resources
  • Protect forests from industrial exploitation
  • Develop sustainable, community-driven economic opportunities

These forests are among the most biodiverse in the United States, yet they face some of the highest logging rates globally. At the same time, the communities living in these regions often experience:

  • High poverty rates
  • Limited access to funding
  • Systemic barriers to land ownership

The JCF addresses these challenges by simplifying access to funding and prioritizing community-led solutions.

Why the Justice Conservation Fund Matters

Traditional conservation funding models often exclude under-resourced communities due to:

  1. Complex application processes
  2. Slow disbursement timelines
  3. Lack of support for land ownership initiatives

Benefits for the Fund

The Justice Conservation Fund fills funding gaps by:

  • Providing accessible funding pathways
  • Supporting community ownership of forests
  • Encouraging long-term environmental stewardship
  • Promoting equitable economic development

The Justice Conservation Model: A New Approach

The Justice Conservation model is grounded in restorative justice principles. It focuses on repairing historical harm while building a more equitable and sustainable future.

Key Principles of the Model

  • Prioritizes communities most affected by industrial logging
  • Addresses historical inequities in land ownership
  • Centers Indigenous and Black leadership
  • Protects forests and wetlands from exploitation
  • Supports sustainable community use of natural resources
  • Promotes nature-based climate solutions
  • Reframes forests as community assets rather than extractive commodities
  • Encourages economic development tied to conservation

Economic and Environmental Impact

The initiative challenges traditional assumptions about forest use by demonstrating that:

  • Outdoor recreation generates five times more jobs than the logging industry
  • Standing forests have 15 times greater economic value than logged forests
  • Healthy ecosystems improve climate resilience by reducing risks from storms and flooding

This approach ensures that conservation is not only environmentally responsible but also economically beneficial.

Real-World Impact: Community Success Stories

1. Pee Dee Indian Tribal Forest (2023)

  • Acquisition of over 75 acres of forestland with wetlands
  • Expansion of tribal land ownership to nearly 100 acres
  • Development of a cultural center open to the public
  • Plans for an organic garden to support food security
  • Increased tourism and cultural education opportunities

This project has become a symbol of hope and self-determination for the community.

2. Brittons Neck Community Forest – “Freedom Land” (2024)

  • 305 acres of mature forest along the Little Pee Dee River
  • Land historically tied to slavery, now reclaimed for descendants
  • Permanent protection through a community trust

Key features of the land include:

  • Old-growth cypress stands
  • Five oxbow lakes
  • Nearly one mile of riverfront

This project represents a powerful act of restorative justice, transforming a painful history into a legacy of empowerment and sustainability.

3. Lower Eastern Cherokee Nation Tribal Forest (Current Project)

  • Purchase of 13.5 acres adjacent to existing tribal land
  • Permanent conservation of ancestral land
  • Protection from industrial threats such as wood pellet facilities

Planned developments:

  • Minimal-disturbance educational trail
  • Interactive learning stations about Cherokee traditions
  • Cultural preservation initiatives

This marks the first project funded directly through the Justice Conservation Fund.

Funding and Financial Structure

  • Over $1,000,000 has been raised to establish the fund
  • The fund is housed in Optus Bank, a Black-owned financial institution
  • Optus Bank is both:
    • A Minority Depository Institution (MDI)
    • A Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI)

This ensures that investments align with community-focused and equitable financial practices.

Organizations Behind the Initiative

Dogwood Alliance

  • 30 years of experience in forest and climate protection
  • Focus on community organizing and advocacy
  • Extensive network across the Southern United States

New Alpha Community Development Corporation

  • Over a decade of experience supporting underserved communities
  • Focus on environmental and economic challenges
  • Strong emphasis on community-led solutions

Together, these organizations are building a conservation model rooted in justice, equity, and sustainability.

Looking Ahead: Expansion and Future Opportunities

The Justice Conservation Fund is expanding beyond South Carolina to serve the broader Southern Coastal Blackbelt region. The demand for funding continues to grow as more communities seek to:

  • Protect their forests
  • Build local economies
  • Preserve cultural heritage

The initiative is actively seeking support to scale its impact and reach more communities in need.

Deadline

Ongoing no fixed deadline

For more information and to apply click here

For more grant opportunities visit OFY here

The post Justice Conservation Fund: Empowering Communities to Protect Forests and Build Sustainable Futures appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: BlueDot Impact Rapid Grants 2026: Get Up to $10,000 Quickly to Power Your AI Safety Project
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
If you are working on a meaningful AI safety idea and need rapid funding to move forward, the BlueDot Impact Rapid Grants program may be exactly what you need. Designed for speed, flexibility, and real-world impact, this funding opportunity removes barriers between talented individuals and the work that matters most. In this comprehensive guide, you […]

The post BlueDot Impact Rapid Grants 2026: Get Up to $10,000 Quickly to Power Your AI Safety Project appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are working on a meaningful AI safety idea and need rapid funding to move forward, the BlueDot Impact Rapid Grants program may be exactly what you need. Designed for speed, flexibility, and real-world impact, this funding opportunity removes barriers between talented individuals and the work that matters most.

In this comprehensive guide, you will learn everything about the Rapid Grants program—from funding scope and eligibility to application steps and tips for success.

 

What Are BlueDot Impact Rapid Grants?

The Rapid Grants program by BlueDot Impact is a fast-track funding initiative that supports individuals and teams working on AI safety projects.

Over the past months, the program has already distributed tens of thousands of dollars to support innovative ideas. Now, it is expanding its scope to fund even more impactful work across the global AI safety ecosystem.

The goal is simple: fund ambitious people doing concrete work that helps ensure AI develops safely and benefits humanity.

 

Funding Overview

Key figures:

  • Grant Amount: Up to $10,000 per project
  • Decision Timeline: Approximately 5 working days
  • Total Grants Awarded: 88+
  • Total Funding Distributed: Over $91,000

This makes the program one of the fastest and most accessible funding opportunities in the AI field today.

 

What Can the Grant Fund?

The program is highly flexible and supports a wide range of activities, including:

  1. Research and Technical Work
  • AI safety research projects
  • Red teaming and evaluation frameworks
  • Policy and governance analysis
  1. Events and Community Building
  • Meetups and event series
  • Workshops and conferences
  • Community chapters in different regions
  1. Travel and Collaboration
  • Travel to AI hubs
  • Conference participation
  • Fieldwork and collaboration visits
  1. Tools and Infrastructure
  • Compute resources
  • API credits
  • Software tools and project infrastructure
  1. High-Impact Experimental Ideas
  • Innovative or unconventional projects
  • Early-stage ideas with strong potential

 

What Makes a Strong Application?

To increase your chances of being funded, your project should:

  • Be concrete and already underway
  • Have a clear bottleneck (e.g., lack of funds for compute, travel, or event costs)
  • Show real potential for impact in AI safety
  • Include specific and justified costs

Good fit examples:

  • A research prototype needing compute credits
  • A local AI safety meetup series needing venue funding
  • A collaboration trip to work with experts

Use caution with:

  • Vague or early-stage ideas without proof of work
  • General-purpose purchases (e.g., laptops, subscriptions)
  • Requests for living expenses (only considered in exceptional cases)

 

How the Application Process Works

The Rapid Grants process is intentionally simple and efficient:

Step 1: Apply
Fill out a short application (takes approximately 5 minutes). Explain your project, funding need, and budget.

Step 2: Get a Decision
Receive a response in about 5 working days.

Step 3: Receive Funding
Funding is often paid upfront. Some cases may involve reimbursement.

Step 4: Execute Your Project
Use the funds as agreed. Notify the team if plans change significantly.

Step 5: Share Your Impact
Provide a short update after completion. No lengthy reports are required.

 

Examples of Funded Projects

The program has already supported diverse initiatives, such as:

  • AI safety community development in Poland
  • Open-source AI regulation tracking platforms
  • Agent-based evaluation tools for AI systems
  • Data center community response tracking

These examples show that both technical and community-driven projects are welcome.

 

Additional Benefits Beyond Funding

Receiving a Rapid Grant does not only mean financial support. You may also gain:

  • Access to a global AI safety community
  • Invitations to exclusive events and programs
  • Opportunities for collaboration and visibility

 

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

Should I apply if I am not sure I qualify?
Yes. If your work is concrete and ongoing, it is better to apply than to self-reject.

Can events and meetups be funded?
Yes. Many funded projects include event series and community activities.

Is travel covered?
Yes, especially if it clearly contributes to your project’s impact.

What if I need more than $10,000?
You can still apply or contact the organization about other funding opportunities.

 

Why You Should Apply

The BlueDot Impact Rapid Grants stand out because they are:

  • Fast – Decisions in just a few days
  • Flexible – Funding a wide range of ideas
  • Accessible – Simple application process
  • Impact-driven – Focused on meaningful AI safety work

If you have a project that just needs a push to move forward, this could be your opportunity.

 

How to Apply

To submit your application, visit the official program page and click “Apply now.”

Clearly explain:

  • What you are building
  • Why it matters
  • What you need funding for

 

Deadline

There is no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, so you may apply at any time.

Learn more about this opportunity on the official BlueDot Impact website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more grant opportunities? Visit the OFY website: Click here

The post BlueDot Impact Rapid Grants 2026: Get Up to $10,000 Quickly to Power Your AI Safety Project appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Bank DIME Recruitment 2026: High-Paying Global Research Jobs, Field Coordinator Roles and Data Science Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Development Impact (DIME) department at the World Bank has officially launched its Spring 2026 recruitment drive, opening doors for highly skilled professionals worldwide. This is a major global hiring initiative targeting Research Assistants, Field Coordinators, and Data Science & Architecture Analysts to support cutting-edge impact evaluation projects across multiple countries. If you are looking […]

The post World Bank DIME Recruitment 2026: High-Paying Global Research Jobs, Field Coordinator Roles and Data Science Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Development Impact (DIME) department at the World Bank has officially launched its Spring 2026 recruitment drive, opening doors for highly skilled professionals worldwide. This is a major global hiring initiative targeting Research Assistants, Field Coordinators, and Data Science & Architecture Analysts to support cutting-edge impact evaluation projects across multiple countries.

If you are looking to build a career in international development, data science, or policy research, this opportunity provides a direct pathway to work on high-impact global projects aimed at reducing poverty and improving livelihoods.

Overview of the Opportunity

The World Bank’s Development Impact (DIME) unit is hiring 9 consultants across multiple roles and projects globally. These professionals will collaborate with leading researchers and policy experts to design, implement, and analyze impact evaluations.

The positions span across:

  • Research Assistants (RA)
  • Field Coordinators (FC)
  • Data Science and Architecture Analysts

These roles are structured as Short-Term Consultant (STC) contracts, with durations varying by project.

What is DIME and Why It Matters

DIME is a research department within the World Bank focused on generating high-quality data and evidence to improve development outcomes. Their work supports governments and institutions by:

  • Designing evidence-based policies
  • Running impact evaluations across sectors
  • Building data systems for decision-making

Key sectors include:

  • Agriculture
  • Governance
  • Climate Change
  • Financial and Private Sector Development
  • Social Protection and Jobs
  • Infrastructure and Transport
  • Fragility, Conflict, and Violence

Available Roles and Responsibilities

1. Research Assistants (RA)

Research Assistants play a critical role in data-driven research. Their responsibilities include:

  • Cleaning and analyzing datasets (household surveys, administrative data, etc.)
  • Writing reproducible code using tools like Stata, R, or Python
  • Managing and integrating multiple data sources
  • Supporting survey design and data collection processes
  • Conducting literature reviews and contributing to reports
  • Preparing replication files for academic research
  • Participating in code reviews and ensuring research transparency
  • Supporting ethical compliance and data security

Some RA roles are Pre-Doctoral positions, ideal for candidates planning to pursue a PhD.

2. Field Coordinators (FC)

Field Coordinators are responsible for managing on-the-ground implementation of research projects. Their duties include:

  • Coordinating between researchers, government officials, and local stakeholders
  • Supervising data collection and survey teams
  • Ensuring adherence to research protocols and timelines
  • Monitoring intervention implementation
  • Conducting quality checks and field audits
  • Managing logistics (devices, survey tools, staffing)
  • Providing regular progress reports
  • Supporting training and capacity building in-country

These roles are typically based in the country of project implementation.

3. Data Science and Architecture Analysts

These roles focus on advanced data systems and analytics:

  • Analyzing large-scale datasets
  • Evaluating and improving data pipelines
  • Identifying inefficiencies in data systems
  • Designing integrated data architectures
  • Exploring new sources of microdata
  • Developing dashboards and automation tools
  • Communicating insights to technical and non-technical stakeholders

Locations and Work Flexibility

  • Research Assistants & Data Analysts: Remote or Washington, DC-based
  • Field Coordinators: Based in project countries

Projects span multiple regions including:

  • Rwanda
  • India
  • Ethiopia
  • Romania and Bulgaria
  • United States (remote-based roles)

Who Can Apply?

Eligible Candidates

This opportunity is open globally. Applicants from all countries and continents are encouraged to apply, especially those with experience in developing countries.

Required Qualifications

Educational Background

Applicants must have:

  • A Master’s degree in one of the following fields:
    • Economics
    • Public Policy
    • Statistics
    • Computer Science
    • Data Science
    • Or a closely related discipline

Technical Skills

For Research Assistants & Field Coordinators:

  • Advanced proficiency in Stata or R
  • Experience with ODK-based survey tools (especially for FC roles)
  • Knowledge of Python (preferred or required for some roles)
  • Familiarity with:
    • Machine Learning
    • Natural Language Processing
    • Geospatial analysis
    • Data visualization tools

For Data Science Analysts:

  • Strong expertise in Python
  • Advanced knowledge of data pipelines and system architecture
  • Experience with large datasets and structured databases
  • Understanding of data integration and interoperability systems

Experience Requirements

  • Strong background in impact evaluation and econometrics
  • Experience with household survey data (for RAs)
  • Field research or data collection management experience (for FCs)
  • Experience working in developing countries is highly preferred
  • Proven ability to work in collaborative and cross-cultural teams

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English (mandatory)
  • Additional languages required for specific roles:
    • French
    • Hindi
    • Portuguese
    • Swahili
    • Amharic
    • Kinyarwanda

Soft Skills

  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to work independently and in teams
  • Cultural sensitivity and adaptability
  • Training and capacity-building experience is an advantage

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a zipped folder containing:

  1. Resume (CV)
  2. Statement of Interest (SOI)
    • Maximum 500 words
    • Separate statements for each role category (RA, FC, etc.)
  3. Code Samples (Optional but recommended)
    • Demonstrating proficiency in:
      • Stata
      • R
      • Python
      • ODK
    • Alternatively, provide GitHub links to specific scripts

Apply here

Selection Process

The recruitment process includes:

  • Initial application screening
  • Technical assessment (for shortlisted candidates)
  • Interview stage

Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

Contract Terms and Benefits

  • Engagement as Short-Term Consultants (STC)
  • Competitive compensation aligned with World Bank standards
  • Opportunity to work on globally recognized research projects
  • Exposure to international policy and development work

Important Notes

  • All work produced belongs exclusively to the World Bank
  • Candidates must not publish project-related materials without permission
  • Applications are treated with strict confidentiality
  • The World Bank strongly encourages diversity and inclusion

Why You Should Apply

This is more than just a job—it’s an opportunity to:

  • Work on impactful global development challenges
  • Collaborate with top researchers and institutions
  • Gain experience in rigorous policy evaluation
  • Strengthen your career in international development and data science

Application Deadline

Wednesday, 29th April 2026 (1:00 PM EDT)

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post World Bank DIME Recruitment 2026: High-Paying Global Research Jobs, Field Coordinator Roles and Data Science Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Oxford University RAIN Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Researchers to Drive AI Innovation in Energy, Agriculture, and Health
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The University of Oxford has officially opened applications for the prestigious RAIN Fellowship 2026, an exclusive programme designed to empower mid-career African researchers working at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence (AI), emerging technologies, and real-world development challenges. Delivered in collaboration with the Africa Oxford Initiative, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to engage in high-impact, […]

The post Oxford University RAIN Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Researchers to Drive AI Innovation in Energy, Agriculture, and Health appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Oxford has officially opened applications for the prestigious RAIN Fellowship 2026, an exclusive programme designed to empower mid-career African researchers working at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence (AI), emerging technologies, and real-world development challenges.

Delivered in collaboration with the Africa Oxford Initiative, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to engage in high-impact, industry-driven research while building global academic partnerships. With a structured blend of remote collaboration and an immersive residency in the United Kingdom, the programme is positioned to accelerate Africa-led innovation across critical sectors.

About the RAIN Fellowship Programme

The RAIN Fellowship is a 12-month programme aimed at bridging the gap between academic research and practical industry application. It focuses on enabling African researchers to transform their ideas into scalable solutions that address pressing societal and economic challenges.

Participants will collaborate with leading academic experts and industry stakeholders to co-develop research projects that are both innovative and implementable.

Fellowship Objectives

The programme is designed to:

  • Strengthen Africa-led research in AI and emerging technologies
  • Promote collaboration between academia and industry
  • Support the development of solutions tailored to African contexts
  • Encourage global partnerships with leading research institutions
  • Translate academic research into real-world applications

Fellowship Duration and Structure

The RAIN Fellowship spans 12 months, combining both remote and in-person engagement:

Key Components:

  1. Collaborative Research Development
    • Fellows partner with Africa-focused industry organisations
    • Research projects are refined to align with real-world challenges
  2. Oxford Residency
    • A 2-month in-person residency at the University of Oxford
    • Direct collaboration with academic mentors and global research networks
  3. Industry Engagement
    • Continuous interaction with industry stakeholders
    • Ensures research outputs are practical and implementable

Fellowship Focus Areas

Applicants must align their research with AI and emerging technologies in one of the following sectors:

1. Energy Sector

  • Smart energy systems
  • Renewable energy innovation
  • Data-driven energy efficiency solutions

2. Agriculture Sector

  • Precision agriculture technologies
  • Climate-resilient farming systems
  • AI-powered productivity tools

3. Health Sector

  • Digital health innovations
  • AI diagnostics and health analytics
  • Technology-driven healthcare delivery

Number of Fellowships

  • Only 5 fellows will be selected for the 2026 cohort, making this a highly competitive opportunity.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be a mid-career researcher affiliated with an African university or research institute
  • Have active research involvement in AI or emerging technologies
  • Focus on Energy, Agriculture, or Health sectors
  • Demonstrate the ability to collaborate across academia and industry
  • Propose research addressing real-world, industry-driven challenges
  • Show leadership potential and commitment to innovation in Africa

Collaboration Requirements

Before applying, candidates are expected to:

  • Identify a collaborator based at Oxford University
  • Define a clear, industry-relevant research focus
  • Demonstrate how the collaboration will enhance research impact

This ensures applicants enter the programme with a well-structured research plan and partnership strategy.

Expected Outcomes

By the end of the fellowship, participants will:

  • Develop impactful, industry-relevant research outputs
  • Strengthen international academic collaborations
  • Build sustainable partnerships between African and global institutions
  • Advance practical AI solutions addressing development challenges

Application Deadline

  • All applications must be submitted by 15 May 2026
  • Late submissions may not be considered

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official fellowship portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to:

  • Prepare a detailed collaboration plan
  • Clearly define their research focus
  • Align proposals with industry needs and fellowship priorities
  • Click here to apply

Why You Should Apply

The RAIN Fellowship offers a rare chance to:

  • Work with world-class researchers and institutions
  • Gain international exposure
  • Contribute to Africa’s technological transformation
  • Turn innovative ideas into real-world solutions

This is more than a fellowship—it’s a platform to shape the future of AI-driven development in Africa.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Oxford University RAIN Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Researchers to Drive AI Innovation in Energy, Agriculture, and Health appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded HER-CARE Doctoral Network 2026 in Europe: Marie Curie PhD Opportunities in Breast Cancer Research
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
If you are an aspiring researcher looking to build a global career in health science, data science, or artificial intelligence, the HER-CARE Doctoral Network 2026 offers a life-changing opportunity. This prestigious programme, funded under the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA), is designed to train the next generation of researchers through fully funded doctoral positions across Europe. […]

The post Fully Funded HER-CARE Doctoral Network 2026 in Europe: Marie Curie PhD Opportunities in Breast Cancer Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are an aspiring researcher looking to build a global career in health science, data science, or artificial intelligence, the HER-CARE Doctoral Network 2026 offers a life-changing opportunity. This prestigious programme, funded under the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA), is designed to train the next generation of researchers through fully funded doctoral positions across Europe.

HER-CARE is focused on advancing research in breast cancer, particularly in prevention, risk prediction, and clinical management. The programme combines cutting-edge science with international collaboration, giving selected candidates access to world-class research environments and industry exposure.

 

What is the HER-CARE Doctoral Network?

The HER-CARE initiative is a highly competitive doctoral training programme that brings together leading universities, research institutions, and industry partners worldwide.

It is part of the MSCA Doctoral Network (MSCA-DN), a European Commission-funded initiative that aims to:

  • Train highly skilled doctoral researchers
  • Promote international mobility
  • Strengthen collaboration between academia and industry
  • Address global scientific and societal challenges

Through this programme, selected candidates will work on innovative breast cancer research projects while pursuing their PhD in a structured and supportive environment.

Why You Should Join HER-CARE

Joining the HER-CARE network offers far more than just a PhD. It is a complete career development experience.

Key Benefits

  1. World-Class Training
    • Access to advanced scientific and technical training
    • Development of transferable skills such as leadership and communication
  2. International Collaboration
    • Work with top researchers across Europe and beyond
    • Engage with both academic and industry experts
  3. Career Development
    • Gain multidisciplinary research experience
    • Participate in mentoring and networking events
    • Opportunities to organize scientific events
  4. Impactful Research
    • Contribute to real-world solutions in breast cancer prevention
    • Help develop personalized treatment and risk prediction strategies

Available Doctoral Positions (2026 Intake)

HER-CARE offers multiple doctoral projects (DC positions) across Europe. Below are some of the currently open opportunities:

Open Positions with Deadlines

  • DC1 – Improving classification of pathogenetic variants
    • Location: Nicosia, Cyprus
    • Deadline: May 11, 2026 (11:00 CET)
  • DC4 – Impact of hormone replacement therapy in BRCA1/2 carriers
    • Location: Vienna, Austria
    • Deadline: April 26, 2026
  • DC6 – Novel risk factors and prediction models for early onset cancer
    • Location: Utrecht, Netherlands
    • Deadline: April 30, 2026
  • DC14 – AI models linking protein signatures to immune changes
    • Location: Cambridge, UK
    • Deadline: April 24, 2026
  • DC15 – Multi-modal models for breast cancer treatment prediction
    • Location: Vienna, Austria
    • Deadline: April 26, 2026

Other Opportunities

  • Several positions are open on a rolling basis or already closed
  • Projects span countries including France, Sweden, Spain, Denmark, and the Netherlands

Each role focuses on different areas such as:

  • Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
  • Bioinformatics and Data Science
  • Epidemiology and Biostatistics
  • Genetics and Biomedical Sciences

Eligibility Requirements

To apply for the HER-CARE doctoral positions, candidates must meet the following conditions:

Core Requirements

  • Must not hold a PhD at the time of recruitment
  • Must not have lived or worked in the host country for more than 12 months in the last 36 months
  • Must be available for full-time commitment
  • Must demonstrate strong English proficiency

These rules are standard for all MSCA Doctoral Network programmes.

Required Skills and Expertise

Depending on the project, applicants may need:

  • Bioinformatics and data science skills
  • Knowledge in epidemiology or statistics
  • Experience in AI or machine learning
  • Background in genetics or biomedical sciences

Some roles may also require:

  • Research publication experience
  • Laboratory or clinical research exposure

Application Process

To APPLY, candidates must follow the instructions for each specific doctoral position.

Typical Application Requirements

  • A well-written motivation letter
  • A detailed CV (including publications, if any)
  • Contact details of at least two referees

Applicants are encouraged to carefully tailor their application to the specific project they are applying for.

Important Deadlines

Deadlines vary by position, but key dates include:

  • April 24, 2026
  • April 26, 2026
  • April 30, 2026
  • May 11, 2026

Candidates are strongly advised to APPLY early, as some positions may close quickly or be filled on a rolling basis.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The HER-CARE Doctoral Network is not just about earning a PhD — it’s about becoming part of a global movement to improve healthcare outcomes.

Participants will:

  • Work on life-saving research
  • Gain international exposure
  • Build long-term careers in academia or industry

This is one of the most prestigious and impactful doctoral opportunities available in Europe today.

The post Fully Funded HER-CARE Doctoral Network 2026 in Europe: Marie Curie PhD Opportunities in Breast Cancer Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Grants 2026: Up to $250,000 to Support Refugee-Led Research and Livelihood Solutions
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Grants 2026 offer up to $250,000 in funding for refugee-led organizations and researchers with lived experience of displacement, supporting innovative research and practical solutions that improve livelihoods for displaced populations and host communities worldwide. As global displacement continues to rise, there is an urgent need for evidence-based solutions that promote […]

The post Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Grants 2026: Up to $250,000 to Support Refugee-Led Research and Livelihood Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Grants 2026 offer up to $250,000 in funding for refugee-led organizations and researchers with lived experience of displacement, supporting innovative research and practical solutions that improve livelihoods for displaced populations and host communities worldwide.

As global displacement continues to rise, there is an urgent need for evidence-based solutions that promote economic inclusion, resilience, and sustainable livelihoods. This initiative—supported by the IKEA Foundation and implemented by Innovations for Poverty Action (IPA) and J-PAL—aims to bridge the gap between research, policy, and practice by funding projects that generate actionable insights and scalable impact.

About the Program

The Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) is a research and funding program designed to advance rigorous evidence on how to improve economic outcomes for displaced populations. It supports a wide range of research stages, from early exploratory work to full-scale impact evaluations and infrastructure development.

The program places strong emphasis on supporting refugee-led organizations and researchers with lived experience, recognizing that those closest to the challenges are best positioned to design meaningful and effective solutions.

DLI funding spans multiple grant categories, including exploratory research, pilot studies, full impact evaluations, infrastructure projects, and evidence use initiatives. Additional travel grants are also available to support participation in academic and policy spaces.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Despite significant investment in displacement-related programs globally, there remains limited high-quality evidence on what truly works to support long-term economic inclusion and resilience. Many interventions lack rigorous evaluation, making it difficult for policymakers and practitioners to scale effective solutions.

This initiative directly addresses that gap by funding research that not only generates knowledge but also influences real-world policies and programs. It encourages interdisciplinary collaboration, innovative methodologies, and the inclusion of marginalized voices in research design and implementation.

By prioritizing lived experience and local leadership, DLI promotes more equitable and context-relevant solutions that can lead to lasting change.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the DLI program gain access to substantial financial resources that enable them to design, test, and scale impactful projects. Beyond funding, the program provides an opportunity to contribute to global knowledge on displacement and livelihoods, influencing policy decisions and development strategies.

Grantees benefit from increased visibility within the global research and policy community, as well as opportunities to collaborate with leading institutions and experts. The initiative also supports dissemination efforts, helping researchers translate findings into actionable insights for governments, NGOs, and international organizations.

Importantly, participants can strengthen their research capacity, build long-term partnerships, and develop solutions that have tangible impact on vulnerable populations.

Who Can Apply

The program is open to researchers, nonprofit organizations, and implementing agencies working on displacement-related issues. Priority is given to refugee-led organizations and researchers who have lived experience of displacement.

Applicants are typically required to collaborate with academic researchers, particularly for larger research projects such as impact evaluations. Multidisciplinary teams and partnerships across regions are strongly encouraged.

Eligible Areas

The DLI Grants are global in scope, with a particular focus on low- and middle-income countries and regions affected by displacement. Projects must address challenges faced by displaced populations and host communities, regardless of geographic location.

Application Process and Deadline

The application process includes multiple stages depending on the grant type. For the 2026 funding round, applicants must first submit a mandatory Expression of Interest by May 15, 2026, followed by a full proposal submission by July 24, 2026.

Applicants are required to submit a detailed research proposal outlining objectives, methodology, expected impact, and relevance to DLI priorities. Supporting documents such as CVs and institutional affiliations are also necessary.

Given the competitive nature of the program, applicants are encouraged to clearly demonstrate the policy relevance, scalability, and evidence-generation potential of their proposed projects.

Read the official call and apply on the IPA website.

Explore more Opportunities

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Displaced Livelihoods Initiative (DLI) Grants 2026: Up to $250,000 to Support Refugee-Led Research and Livelihood Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust Grant 2026: Complete Guide to Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits in the United States
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust is now inviting applications from qualified nonprofit organizations seeking funding support for impactful community initiatives in 2026. This grant program offers structured application cycles throughout the year, giving eligible organizations multiple opportunities to apply for financial assistance to support charitable projects within approved U.S. regions. With clearly defined submission windows, […]

The post F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust Grant 2026: Complete Guide to Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust is now inviting applications from qualified nonprofit organizations seeking funding support for impactful community initiatives in 2026. This grant program offers structured application cycles throughout the year, giving eligible organizations multiple opportunities to apply for financial assistance to support charitable projects within approved U.S. regions.

With clearly defined submission windows, eligibility requirements, and funding timelines, this opportunity is designed to support 501(c)(3) organizations committed to meaningful social impact.

Grant Application Cycles and Key Deadlines

The Trust operates two annual application cycles:

Cycle 1: March – June

  • Letter of Intent (LOI) Window: March 1 – May 31
  • Full Application Deadline: June 30
  • Funding Distribution: August 15 – September 1

Cycle 2: September – December

  • Letter of Intent (LOI) Window: September 1 – November 30
  • Full Application Deadline: December 31
  • Funding Distribution: February 15 – March 1

All applications must be submitted online through the official grant portal. Late submissions or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Application Process Overview

The application process consists of three main steps:

1. Registration

Organizations must register on the Trust’s online portal. Returning applicants are already registered and can proceed using existing credentials.

2. Letter of Intent (LOI)

Applicants submit a brief proposal outlining their project. The Trust reviews submissions within 2–3 weeks to determine eligibility for the next stage.

3. Full Grant Application

If the LOI is accepted, applicants complete a full application before the cycle deadline.

Final funding decisions are typically made approximately two months after each application deadline during Advisory Board meetings held in February and August.

Important Funding Restrictions

Applicants must carefully review eligibility criteria before applying:

  • Grants are limited to organizations operating in the United States
  • Primary funding regions include:
    • Florida
    • Massachusetts
    • New Jersey
    • New York
    • South Carolina
  • Organizations must hold valid 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status
  • Funding is not provided for:
    • Legal aid programs
    • Loans or debt financing
    • Moving expenses
    • Public policy or advocacy groups
    • Surveys or research studies
    • Grants to individuals
  • International organizations and overseas project funding are not eligible
  • Prior grantees must wait 18 months before reapplying

Application Guidelines and Requirements

  • Applications are accepted only online
  • Email or mailed submissions are not permitted
  • All documents must use alpha-numeric file names only
  • Contact information must remain up to date throughout the process
  • Progress reports are required every six months after funding approval
  • The full process may take up to six months from application to final decision

Why This Grant Matters

This funding opportunity is ideal for nonprofits seeking:

  • Sustainable funding for community development projects
  • Support for educational, health, and social programs
  • Long-term partnership opportunities with a charitable trust
  • Structured, recurring grant cycles for annual planning

Early application is strongly recommended, as late submissions may not allow sufficient time for completion of the review process.

How to Apply

Organizations can begin by registering on the official F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust grant portal and submitting a Letter of Intent during the active application window. For assistance, applicants may contact: kelly@fjbrothertoncharitabletrust.org

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here.

Discover more grants opportunities, click here.

Conclusion

The F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust grant program provides a valuable funding pathway for eligible nonprofit organizations across select U.S. states. With two annual cycles, a structured review process, and significant funding potential, this opportunity supports mission-driven organizations committed to long-term community impact.

The post F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust Grant 2026: Complete Guide to Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
